title: rosa micrantha subvar. plicata
intro: Rosa micrantha subvar. plicata, also known as the dwarf alpine rose, is a compact and fragrant flowering plant native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its small, pink to white flowers and dark green, glossy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Rosa micrantha subvar. plicata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
